text.title: GitHub Actions — Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Workflow MUST declare explicit `on:` with branch + path filters for push/PR. Avoid the no-filter default — it burns minutes on irrelevant changes.
rule.rationale: Default triggers fire on every commit anywhere; explicit filters keep CI focused on the work it should verify.

rule.statement: Workflow MUST declare `permissions:` at workflow level with minimum scopes. The default (write-all) is rejected even for basic workflows.
rule.rationale: Even basic workflows benefit from scoped tokens; starting with default-write-all builds bad habits.

rule.statement: Canonical sequence: checkout (pinned) → setup-runtime → cache → install → run command. Cache enabled on the dependency step.
rule.rationale: Skipping cache wastes 60-180s per run; unpinned checkout is a supply-chain risk.

rule.statement: Successful builds MUST upload a meaningful artifact (test report, binary, html report) with retention ≤30d.
rule.rationale: Artifacts make CI useful as evidence, not just a green/red light.

rule.statement: Workflow file MUST have an owner comment + changelog notes in PRs that change it. Anonymous workflow files become orphans within a year.
rule.rationale: When the workflow breaks at 3am, the owner comment tells the on-call who to wake.

rule.statement: Skip when repo already has a v1.1.0+ workflow OR tests don't run OR no owner exists for the file.
rule.rationale: Below threshold the basics methodology is redundant; without prereqs it cannot execute.
